review_text: Booked a powered site for a couple of days.Was surprised to find this quite nice caravan park next to the Erskine River. Foodworks just across the road,take a stroll to the Swing Bridge,walking distance to all the shops and restaurants.

Super camping parfaitement équipé, au bord d’une rivière avec des perroquets partout !
Le staff est aussi super accueillant
Merci, nous reviendrons !

My husband and I had a lovely 2 night, one full day stay in Riverside Family Cabin 5 recently in Lorne. Lorne itself is still in recovery mode since the major flooding in January 2026, and access to several waterfalls was limited. The local pier, another local attraction was closing on the afternoon we arrived for major repairs.

That said we still loved what we saw and did and our cabin had all we needed and was in a nice and quiet spot of the park. I would have given it 5/5 for room if the positioning of the cabin hadn’t meant that one end of our deck looked directly into the living area of Cabin 4 beside us. All it needs is a privacy screen erected at the end of this part of the deck to create a better sense of privacy. Fortunately no one was using it until our last night but even that was a touch un-nerving. The cabins are very close!
Other than that we appreciated the closeness to a coffee shop, pizza place, both small and artisanal, nestled next a gorgeous gift and women’s wear store. This little nest of shops was about 300m away. The Riverside site is also just across the road from the Information Centre and a Foodworks. Lorne Foreshore Caravan Park was a great choice for us.

Expensive for what you get. Was raining when we stayed so very sloppy mud and bark can’t really handle weight of the car & caravan. No pets aloud over Easter. Small playground for the kids but not much else. Amenities are basic, could use a clean though. Stopped for 1 night to see lorne

Alquilamos un site para la campervan, una noche. Es lindo! Tiene acceso a la playa, es amplio y está cerca del centro!
Los baños están bien pero podrían mejorarse.
Ocean Road powered site, looks like a getto....worst camp site ever...

Dreadful campground.. looks like it hasn't been maintained in many years. Reception not open. Only a small entrance, hard to get through. Overhanging tree's.. backing onto the supermarket.., concrete wall with graffiti, listening to the refrigerators from the supermarket..🙄🤷..$70 a night. I was hesitant to book it based on the poor reviews, but we wanted to see Lorne, thankfully only one night.
Fabulous spot. Can walk everywhere. Right near supermarket. Very central but very quiet.

2026年2月8日に1人で自転車で行きました。グーグルマップからQueens Park Caravan ParkのHPに入って予約しましたが、予約完了のメールはLorne Foreshore Caravan Parkなっていました。選択した宿泊種類によってキャンプ場が違っていました。
施設はきれいに手入れされており問題はありません。シャワーの温度も水量も十分でした。自転車で5分ほどの所にスーパーマーケットがあり便利です。ただ、他の方も書いていますが、使用料が高く思います。一番安い電源なし、キッチンなしでも1泊54ドルでした。オーストラリヤのキャンプ場は、1人でも6人で同じ料金なので1人旅は高額になってしまいます。

The facility was better than expected and exceptionally clean. I really liked that access to the facilities is controlled by a code, which makes you feel safe and secure. The entire site is very clean and tidy, with a cleaner coming in each morning. Washing up
Liquid, cleaning sponges, cloths, and towels are provided, which is a thoughtful touch.

The shower room is well equipped with hooks for towels, and the shower itself is powerful. The kitchen facilities are excellent, with a kettle, toaster, air fryer, microwave, and BBQ available.

My only complaint is that the site is not clearly signposted where the pitches are located. I ended up driving around narrow lanes trying to find my pitch and had to leave the site to turn around before attempting again. The site itself is a dirt track with grass pitches.

I had booked a powered pitch, but it wasn’t clear how to access the electricity, as I couldn’t find instructions on how and where to connect to the power supply.
The worst caravan park we have ever stayed at, the place should be shut down by the health department due to the state of amenities. DO NOT STSY HERE!

Was our last overnight stop on the Great Ocean Road. Expensive and reflected as there was only a handful of campers, even on a busy weekend. Great and clean facilities. Difficult navigating the park finding your allocated spot on arrival. Signage will eliminate this issue altogether. Friendly staff 😊
A shitty, cramped campsite with squawking cockatoos. Don't bother. Lorne itself is lovely.

The cabin was well equipped and very clean. The location of the cabin is directly opposite the delivery entrance for the supermarket I was woken up between 6 and 6.30 each morning. No mention of this when I booked I was looking for a quiet few days. I would not stay here again.

Great location, close to the main road of Lorne (walking distance). But I’m disappointed with the actual park itself. Stayed in the dog friendly part and there’s so much more potential to the park itself. There could be concrete sites, fairy lights in the trees even etc and the back of the food shop looks bad and industrial, it ruins the vibe of it (could maybe have artwork on it or something to cover it up). It just needs things to give it that wow. It just has basic level grounds -bathrooms.
Great central location. Toilet facilities ok but need a little TLC. Very quiet site with easy access to beach and town. Large powered sites and easy turning.

Was a great location in terms of being near the beach and the town of lorne.
Only issue we had was the spot we were in (site 21 in top bank) is that it is the beginning of a public road and the cars driving in come in way too fast as the speed hump is further down. They need more signage or a speed hump before the sites start as someone will get hit. We were on edge the whole time wi the a 6yo wanting to ride his bike all weekend
Rustige camping

Nice campsite, located close to the shop and town. Lovely views near the river. Amenities were okay, laundry needs a good clean, I had to clean the washing machine before use. Staff were nice and helpful.
Un joli cadre pour passer quelques jours, mais il faudrait moderniser les parties communes et faire quelque chose contre les moustiques

Lorne itself was amazing — such a beautiful spot with everything close by! The only challenge was check-in and out, as the gate didn’t work properly both times. But once inside, it was worth it. The location couldn’t be better, right in the heart of Lorne! 🌴☕

Brought my own room, so 5 stars. It’s a tent.
Made my own dinner.. 5 star service.
Location is 5 stars, nestled between the river and the beach, this is great little spot to stop.
Great view, right by the river. Nice and quiet, quite affordable, but had to walk quite a ways to the facilities 😅
Place is dated but still clean and comfortable. Nice stay. Easy walk to the main shopping area.
The site was right on river lots of birds and ducks what wonderful stop. We will be back soon

We stayed in a 2 Bedroom Riverview Family Cabin for just 1 night- Clean, Compact, Comfortable, Quiet, Cozy stay in the woods with ensuite bathroom, and car parking just by d cabin, with basic cutleries, pots & pans, chopping board, electric stove, microwave, toaster, kettle, hot & cold water shower with shower gel, towels, 2 bunk beds & a double bed, dining table with chairs, perfect for a family of 4. The only thing Not available is the dishwashing liquid.but thats ok, we can get it frm the supermarket nearby.

Perfect for living with Nature, late checkin via sms for pincode to unlock the safe outside the office, to collect your keys inside. Our cabin located just by d river bank is d best way to feel aligned with nature. We saw a mother Duck and her ducklings strolling together in a pack and were even greeted in the morning, perched on our Balcony a beautiful Bird not sure wat it is tho....

Theres even a bbq pit just outside our cabin! And most important theres Foodworth Supermarket to get basic food essentials which we could cook with in our cabin. Compact apartment, but cozy and we got all d essentials we needed.

Hopefully next time we could stay longer to experience living in the outback. Beisdes  the supermarket, theres even a Halal Lorne Fish & Chips is just a 2 mins drive away ...and their menu is extensive!

Wir waren für eine Nacht auf diesem Campingplatz. Da wir etwas später ankamen konnten wir alle nötigen Informationen (PINs, Stellplatznummer) in einem Briefumschlag an der Rezeption abholen. Die Sanitären Einrichtungen für die Campinggäste waren sauber, allerdings aufgrund des Wetters und fehlender Beheizung etwas kalt. Wir hatten einen Stellplatz direkt am Fluss. Ein gut ausgestatteter Supermarkt war wenige Minuten fußläufig erreichbar.

We arrived at the site after office hours, but a kind staff member assisted us with check-in. The cabin was clean, fully functional, and had a well-equipped kitchen for its size. Our family had a restful night. Wishing your business continued success.

The size of the campsites are laughable. Our 6person tent basically took up the entire size of our campsite. And our tent isn’t even that big (photo attached).The only place we could park our car was in front of our tent. But that barely even fit and I have a tiny hatch. Top bank site is on a public road, lots of cars driving past very close to the tents, so you can imagine it gets really dusty. The very basic amenities available at top bank are clean. No access to kitchen at the main site either. The campsite was practically empty but we got given the site that was right near the Lorne visitor centre, instead of further in, away from the public. Actually ridiculous amount of money to pay for something that could really have been free camping.

The only plus side is the location which is right in the heart of Lorne.however I seriously recommend booking a campsite further away that is cheaper, gives you more room and not on a public road.
Great place

Unfortunately this park is not safe for kids or pets at all, in fact it’s dangerous and we had an horrific experience, we will not return. We submitted a report and heard nothing back in return, no safety measure improvements. Public access to roads near tents are unsafe and cars drive too fast. The road should be for campers only. It is not signed well enough and no security at all. Do not risk your kids or pets, I hope nobody else experiences the terrible situation we had during school holidays.
Very basic sites, on poorly lit location, take a torch with you to the toilets and a large dog
Facilities poor and no kitchen area

We booked at gday Park at  Lorne hoping for the standed we had been used to in this chain.The sites were  terrible, mud and ,mulch was everywhere,site were small, tight to get into.worst caravan park we have stayed in throught our journey and the dearest on top. Very disappointed.,we have travelled from Queensland ,through the outback  to the Victoria then Northern  Road , all the way back to NS W so we have stayed in  many parks.
An amazing place to stay and the staff are super friendly, check it out 👍
Clean amenities, can only get in with a code. Shower has hot water. Very Close to your Neighbors thought.

Municipal site in a great location. Walk to the beach via the sawbridge for sunrise coffees! Best imo are the riverside powered sites by the middling though clean amenities with jaded camp kitchen but you get what you pay for! Fish from your ‘doorstep’.

Despite the park being mostly empty in the middle of winter, we were assigned the site closest to the main road and the park entrance, making it noisy and less safe for our 18m old. There were plenty of sites available so allocating arguably the WORST site in the whole park felt like a slap in the face.
No dump point in this section either, had to go to another section to empty the devil’s esky.
All the 1-star reviews are true.
The reception was uncultured and rude, and the facilities were terrible. All the 1-star reviews are true.

We had a lovely stay at Lorne Foreshore Caravan Park! The location is unbeatable just a short walk to the beach, shops, and restaurants. The park was clean, family friendly, and peaceful even during the busy season.

Our site was spacious, and the amenities were well-maintained. The kids loved the nearby playground and we enjoyed evening walks along the river. Staff were friendly and helpful, making check-in smooth and stress free.

Highly recommend for families, couples, or anyone looking to relax and enjoy the beauty of the Great Ocean Road. We’ll definitely be back!
What a great spot by the inlet. Highly recommend this little gem. Amenities were cleaned daily to a high standard. We’ll definitely be back!
We got a flat battery and they wouldn’t help us, bizarre for a caravan park! Luckily other campers came to the rescue

I have been here several times. And just love the location. Next to the river  Close to everything , beautiful and peacefull. With so many bird visitors .🥰❤️
Very clean amenities. And they allow  fire pits which is great.

Only stayed a night but enjoyed it. Bathrooms are adequate showers were hot with good water pressure but was quite dark inside. Was the most expensive park on our travels, but that goes with territory on the south Victorian coast. Excellent look out and the falls are a must. Lots of food options in town which is a short walk away

Short walk to main street.
Supermarket over the road.
Good coffee nearby, walking distance.
Beautiful spot on Erskine River

Stayed in a cabin , hospital bed sheets very uncomfortable,  those cheap white towels that don't even dry you properly, the toilet paper was so thin you end up using half a roll just to wipe without it breaking. The kids play equipment had dog poo right at the base of the slide. It's a great location and views but the cutting of corners to make a extra buck on amenities makes it not a great place to stay
Great location near beach and a short walk into town.Very expensive compared to other caravann parks in the region. Unclean facilities - bird droppings throughout Ladie's toilets and showers.
Beautiful atmosphere and very quiet for its location
Great location

Most expensive campground we stayed at. Maybe part of being late arrival, maybe not. Do with that info what you will.
There´s another part of this campground, but here is the office. So we drove back and forth to even get here. It was really crowded even off season. Didn´t look all that great either. Granted we stayed at the weekend. The facilities were alright.
My bedroom is bigger than the cabins and the bed along with the sheets have dirt on them. Would not recommend spending hard earned money here.
Stayed for a night. Camp thumbs 👍

First two nights on a reasonable pitch were fine. Staff friendly but 71$ a night pricey. No camp kitchen.  Requested extra night as Labor Day weekend and other sites full. Charged 122$ (no typo error) for a 7x4m gravel pitch 64% increase. Manager refunded 30$ but still extortionate pricing. Loos weren’t cleaned. Rubbish on ground. Unsupervised children tearing around site- running, on bikes and electric scooters. Playground next to amenities open all the time so children playing 6.30am until very late. Used the clothes drying area as a cricket pitch. Limited parking so cars blocking footpaths. Neighbours in tents had loud music and disco lights until after 11.30pm. Avoid! They have a monopoly in Lorne and probably couldn’t care less about bad reviews.
We had the worst site for $72.00per night. The facilities were in need of  lot work. Would not recommend this park for my dogs let alone caravans.
Great location clean tidy and comfortable
Very convinient campsite located near the big market and many shops in Lorne. But each of site is very close and the ground is just a sand so it is bad. And the price is little bit expensive.
We loved Lorne, beautiful place, beaches and a cool little town. Stayed in our camper overnight on the Erskwine river at the park. Expensive for a powered site we thought.

We stayed on a powered camping site in the dog friendly area, worst caravan park we’ve been to but also the most expensive. Tiny sites, cramped, at the back of a foodworks loading dock, unsightly with no views and lots of long term stays. Amenities were OK but no camp kitchen. Had beers stolen from our esky overnight. The location is great to walk into town but we camp to sit outside and enjoy the serenity, none here haha. Wouldn’t stay again and definitely wouldn’t recommend.
Cabin was cool as they had left the AC on. Thanks guys. Great seating near river for drinks.

Nice and peaceful, not many people around, cleanish ammenities, suited my needs nicely. Be careful of the jack jumpers, they weren't too happy to have me there. |It would be nice if people could clean up after themselves, the blobs of toothpaste left in every sink was a bit gross, would you treat your sink at home like that?
The surrounding scenery is also very beautiful, and it is always magnificent and a tribute to the greatness of nature.
Very good location and convenient for everyone

Packed like sardines. Lovely area, just not what we are use to when camping, no room. We couldn't even set up an annexe or gazebo onsite because of the space. Is what it is, but definitely go a little further out next time to a bigger camp spot. Not really for young kids. No room to play etc.

The Queens Park campground was amazing. Great facilities, location and views. Away from the hustle and bustle, it is situated slightly out of the main town centre, however only takes 15-20 minutes to walk into town. We loved it and will be back again.
Stayed in the caravan park. Great location, very quiet, good amenities. Our dog loved it, close to the beach.

Beautiful town and beaches!
The camping is strange, it’s in different locations and although everyone seems to be too close to someone else it’s actually in different levels so it’s ok. Everything is clean and there obviously some spiders. They don’t have bag of ice so it’s good to know.

This camp site is located in five plots on both sides of the river. Plots are grass and gravel so not suitable if the weather is anyway wet. Facilities were standard and clean. Expensive for the limited facilities. Central location a plus.
Nice setting
Prices nearly double on busy periods. Basic amenities, mean spirited cancellation and minimum booking policies. If there was an option I would use it but they have a monopoly and abuse it.

Extremely unfair & unnecessary booking policies! My husband & I & all of our extended family & friends have been re-booking the Lorne Foreshore Caravan park for 35+ years and much to our disappointment, loyalty means nothing to management as the new illogical ‘4 night minimum’ applied for in consecutive long weekends in Melbourne like Melbourne cup long weekend have now stopped us from booking our preference of 3 nights. Like most, the cost of living is outrageous & camping WAS accessible for us, cost & time wise. However, now is no longer an option due to the greed, ill decision & lack of knowledge of management at Great Ocean Road Caravan Parks. Honestly so disappointed after how many years/generations have kept this tradition in our family. Will no longer recommend this location and others connected to it. How many families/individuals are you FORCING to pay for 4 nights who can’t meet that requirement and end up coughing up the funds and leaving it arriving late/early and you know that’s what has to be done for these people to keep up their yearly stays. Absolute joke & rip off!

For a start my review of top bank is as per others descriptions of the top bank. Zero stars.||||There is one rule for locals and another for campers. Campers have their dogs on leash and drive at 10km/hr. Local do what they want and for us that meant them driving at speed 5cm from our tent to avoid the speed humps kicking up dust meaning we could not make or eat food at the site without eating dust. ||||Dogs - we had one local’s dog come and attack our leashed dogs and the owner was nowhere to be seen. Then later he turned up in his car after I was screaming for his dog to go away and stop attacking mine. ||||No one staying in the top bank felt safe with everyone locking everything up whenever their back was turned. ||||Toilets. Was very uncomfortable having men in the ladies amenities. The amenities were the worst I’ve ever experienced with nesting birds pooing over everything and swooping. My husband said the Mens didn’t have birds but that all the fixtures were broken. ||||On our final day the manager called me to call him back urgently. Turned out the ‘park’ was to be graded starting at 8am. When I called back I was asked if I wanted to move to another park. Our setup is hard so I said no but that I’d take a refund for the inconvenience. This never happened as apparently they were only going to be setting up and blocking off access at 8am not actually working. But we left at 8am anyway as we didn’t want to be stuck there unable to get out.||||For one of the most expensive parks in the country it’s an absolutely disgrace. Stay somewhere else.

Before you think about booking here read the other one star reviews here and the reviews on TripAdvisor. Everything they say about the top bank is true.

If you want to have locals driving their cars at speed 5 centimetres from your tent to avoid speed bumps, kicking up dust so you can’t cook or eat on your site, if you want to have your leashed dogs attacked by local dogs running though while the owner drives the car behind them and if you like being swooped by birds in the shower and have no where to put your clean clothes then this is the place for you.

If you haven’t read the reviews before booking and are reading them now, wondering why you paid the most in fees you’ve ever paid only to be camping on a dirt thoroughfare, then leave your own review and in the meantime lock everything up.

One star as XERO stars isn’t an option to choose.

We were told on arrival that we were in a site on the "Top Bank"!!! We had no idea what that was until we were directed back onto the road & around the corner.|It seems this caravan park (& I use that term loosely) is split into different areas, so make sure you know what your site is before booking. Actually, scrap that, just avoid altogether as there has to be much better options out there.                                                              "Top Bank" is literally a public access dusty dirt track, for anyone to access...no privacy, no security, no view so don't be fooled by the photo on the home page of their website. This is NOT a caravan park by any stretch of the imagination. Thankfully only one night for us. We have lapped the country a couple of times & this one rates amongst the worst.

Firstly, Lorne is such a beautiful town. It is so relaxed and there seems to be everything you could want/need/get here. Lots of choices for food – I did pizza twice, fish and chips, burgers, and something else I can’t recall. The pizza place down near the bridge is very good. I stayed up on the beach side, camping in a tent at a powered site. Neighbours came and went and were mostly spread out, so they didn’t have to deal with my (reported and rumoured) snoring. The amenities block was always clean, and the shower was nice and hot. Great place to stay.
Just having on camper site
Can't get much better location. On the river next to the beach and supermarket. Nice walk into town alone the main street or beach. Kids enjoyed the playground and made some caravan park friends.
Me and my family had a great night sleep but unfortunately the cabins were a bit unattended. Apart from that we had a great holiday and we loved fishing in the river
Lovely little caravan park. We stayed in a cabin, and were lucky enough to be right on the river front, so it was a gorgeous view. Hosts were quite friendly.

We opted for Aireys Inlet originally to base ourselves to explore but found it too quiet for us - so even though the reviews on Apollo werent great - we moved here for convenience.  The park was quiet and right on the river - i get if you have kids there is nothing at the park to do - but you are smack bang in town, walking distance to everywhere so would highly recommend.  Only comment would be to steer clear in the warmer months as can only imagine the noise and chaos - but a cracking spot to go in the cooler months!

Average service on check in
Not designed for big vans
Sites are all on bark so is a bit yuk in rain
Toilets were not real clean considering park was almost empty
Great sites right on the Erskine River

Fantastic location on the river. Cosy and warm. Amazing wildlife.Thoroughly enjoyed our stay. A shortwalk to main shopping area with great cafes and Foodworks a 2 minute walk.
My only suggestions would be to put a shelf or caddy in the shower (instead of having to put toiletries on the floor), and some shelves for clothing. This would be very handy for longer stays, instead of having to leave clothes in suitcases.
Nice place to stay as it is close to the town centre, supermarket.
Although we came late, they still serve us to check in smoothly. Toilet was clean, kitchen also well equipped. The park is face to a river with duckling and birds. Good environments.

The cabin we stayed in was a bit older and not equipped for usb connections unless you have an adapter that goes in the wall. Clean but small, and if you're only going to sleep in the cabin, going on outings during the day and evening it is perfect. The staff are great. Friendly and helpful when you need it. I'd stay again but with a caravan next time.
We will be back. Very comfortable and warm and clean. Perfect for our needs.
Stayed at a campsite over an event weekend; a nice and quiet location, good location in vicinity to shops and the Main Street. Easy walk for beach access. Would stay here again!
Did I visit
Holiday heaven.
Lovely spot, quiet location,good facilities and friendly check in. Great park like setting and the soothing sound of the sea to relax you

We were told that we couldn't stay on the seafront as they wasn't dog friendly. The website says they are dog friendly in off peak. So we went to book in and the reception staff said you should read the small print. So we paid an extortionate amount of 52 dollars as we are members of gday camps. We asked if we could have a late checkout and the guy explained that it was subject to availability. We went it our site 101 and low and behold we were the only ones on the site. We were poorly treated and we would definitely not stay at this site again. Toilets and showers were not clean. This is the worst site we had stayed on in 33 days. Travelled from WA so we have stayed on a few.

While the location is amazing, the facilities were filthy. Toilets and showers had bird poop all over the place and had not been properly cleaned for a long time. It was hard to find a clean place to put my clothes.
For comparison I would rate the public toilets at the entry of the park higher than the locked facilities in the park.
For the price it was awful. Would not come back.

We stayed on a powered site in the Erskine river park and it was a beautiful setting. Facilities were really good and a small playground for the kids. Very close to town and supermarket. The staff member on check in was very accommodating and friendly, would definitely stay here again.

Depends where you end up.
We were lucky to score the upper side close to town and the beach.
The amenities are next to public amenities, and they are both very average. Our site was very small and tight, plus no parking for car accept across from our site.
An amazing place full of animals and great views. The river is nearby and animals are everywhere. Good affordable place.
Great position close to the beach. Nice playground for the kids.

Lorne caravan Park is split into 4 areas, so make sure you know where your site is as foreshore is literally a public access dirt rd, that anyone can use and many caravaners did for the dump, this is not a caravan park, no privacy, security, poor.
Ein sehr schöner Camping mit sehr guten sanitär Anlagen direkt am Fluss. Bis lorne Zentrum nur ein paar Gehminuten. Sehr empfehlenswert
我们是晚到了，过了check in时间，但是没有收到自助信箱的密码邮件。之前预定时还特地问过，确认过会发邮件。在信箱前发呆了20分钟，遇到第二个来check in的女士，帮助我拿到了钥匙，否则真要在车里住一晚了么？地理位置很好，一早起来步行了五分钟就到海边，日出很美

We were a little concerned after reading other reviews which suggested that site was cramped but were very happy with our stay at the park. Clearly others have said they had problems but our pitch was easily big enough for out campervan (7m x 2m), we could have parked a car next to it. The facilities were clean and the best thing was the closeness to the town of Lorne which has a lovely beach and a good range of bars and restaurants. I have deducted 1 point only because our pitch no longer had any grass (presumably because it is so late in the season) and the site we were on was behind a supermarket (fantastic for shopping but not great for the view).

We had a powered campsite that was way too small, the sites were very crowded together & uneven so quite a challenge to park our 16 ft Van safely. The park was sadly in need of regular maintenance & tlc. Lorne was a lovely spot & it wouldn’t take too much effort to improve the Foreshore Caravan Park, some money time & effort. The office person was helpful it’s just that the Park just didn’t deliver.

Staying at Ocean Park and we are squished in like sardines if you have a bigger caravan over 20ft go elsewhere. We couldn’t get our caravan onto the site due to bins and poles, so now we have to park our car where the awning would go out and no awning which isn’t comfortable for a 4 night stay!!
No camp kitchen, Very expensive to park on a bit of dirt

나와 아내는 이곳에서 오토캠핑을 하루 하였습니다. 넓은 유료캠핑장인데 노파워 지역이 하루에 54달러 입니다. 바로 옆에 바닷가가 있고
경치가 좋은곳입니다.

I recently stayed at the ocean road section of the Lorne foreshore caravan park, camping with my kids and another family. While the actual foreshore part of the caravan park may be nice, the ocean road section behind the food works is not.
I do not recommend that part of the park at all!
The actual sites were tiny, we were camping in a tent, the other family were in a caravan. We were put right near the entrance/exit to the park, with the bins, toilet block and caravan waste dump point right near us and there was no camp kitchen for us to use at this site. Luckily we were only staying two nights so were able to improvise and not cook. If you're camping in tents do not book this site.
It was noisy, the air con on top of food works runs all night, and there were a number of residents going in and out at all hours of the night and early morning.
Late Sunday night we got an awful sewerage smell engulf our tent from the caravan dump point.
The garbage bins right next to us were emptied just before 7am Tuesday morning, the truck driver decided halfway through emptying the bins he'd have a lengthy conversation with one of the park residents while his noisy truck sat there idling next to our tent.
There is way too much lighting for tents to camp here, it was so light in our tent we didn't even need to use our lantern.
Very disappointing, this place is overrated and I'd definitely be driving straight through onto somewhere nicer if you can't book in the actual foreshore of the park.
Photos of how bright our tent was at night and of how close to the amenities we were including the main thoroughfare to the right of the photo (not shown).
We've come here a few times and we love it. The cabins are great as well as the camp site. The bath and toilet is locked with a code and so clean 👌

Stayed here one night booked a van site which turned out to be a just a patch of dirt very unkept. Small cramped and nowhere to park your car if we put awning out. For $74 per night it’s a rip off would not recommend.

Sadly this beautiful  areas cabins fell short due not being properly cleaned. The floors werent cleaned properly .  A coffe cupmhad coffee rings innit and had to be washedd. A small opened milk was left for our use. The bunkbed was not made up for me and if there was 18 inches probably far less between each bunkbed I would be surprised. I could not sit up in bed . I was on the bottom bunk and had to roll out and get on my knees to stand up. The bathroom was not cleaned properly and the fan was covered un dust. Very busy road to get across to to get to the supermarket . Also the wifi wouldnt work for me even after going to the office and getting it to work  there. As soon as returning to cabin it wouldnt work

Das Office des Lorne Foreshore Caravan Park befindet sich im Ort; der von uns gewählte Queens Caravan Park ist einer von mehreren Camps, die hier verwaltet werden. Das Campinggelände am Hang ist mangels Wegweiser schwierig zu finden.|Wir hatten den Platz 21 mit freiem Blick auf das Meer.  Die Aussicht war es auch, die uns diesen Platz zu einem der besten auf der Tour machten. Zudem waren wir auf diesem Areal bis auf einen Camper mit Zelt alleine. |Wenig ansprechend war der Bereich der dürftig ausgestatteten Camperküche und die BBQ-Herde draussen boten nicht genug Power, um dort richtig zu grillen. |Vom Platz aus erreicht man in relativ kurzer Zeit Wanderwege wie den Ocean Walk, den Tramway Track oder Teddys Lookout. |Häufige Gäste sind die neugierigen Sulphur crested Cockatoos, die durchaus in der Lage sind, die Mülltonnen zu öffen.

After my wife took a turn and was ambulance to Geelong hospital for scans and overnight we we offered a free nights stay. Staff genuinely care.
Fantastic location next to the river and close to town. Very grateful!

So I recently stayed at lorne foreshore caravan park with my mother and this has so far been the worst camping experience of my life. These are some details what me and my mum had to endure, number 1 incredibly overpriced site at $139 for one night CAMPING. 2, the site was not actually a site it was a piece of grass infested by nests of jack jumpers and not large enough for a car and and A SMALL TENT. 3 our tents were bordering the gravel road.4 cars passing the site day and night practically brushing our TENT I was scared for my life that I was going to get run over. 5 drunken campers returning to their campsite opposite our camp site at 3:45am moving their vehicle (for some unknown reason) nearly running me over again and I was so scared I couldn’t go back to sleep. 6 my mother asked the park management what they could do about the Jack jumper plague and they said they couldn’t do anything! whilst we both got bitten four times puting up our tent potentially developing a severe allergy in the process. DO NOT EVER VISIT THIS PARK ITS THE WORSE IN AUSTRALIA WITH UNCARING AND UNHELPFUL MANAGEMENT!

Very dissappointed with our stay. We were crammed in like sardines.  Barely enough room for tent and car. Situated on the end of a cravel road where we had dust all over us from cars multiple times of the day and night.
Bins were overflowing which brought rubbish around us as the cocatoos had a field day attacking the bins. Very noisy. Was kept up till 2am with people partying.
The amenities were cleaned regularly which was great. Ocean views past the trees were breathtaking.
Lorne shops ridiculously over priced.
Have stayed at many caravan parks but this didn't meet expectations.  Will not be returning.
Great place to stay. Toilets were clean and up-to-date. Location is great and an easy walk to the town centre. Will be going back soon.

I recently stayed at Queenspark site with family and friends from 23/12/23 and unfortunately, our experience was marred by unexpected weather conditions (Storm) The adverse weather made it challenging for us to enjoy our planned activities, especially with elderly parents and small children in our group.

What disappointed me further was the interaction with the staff when I approached them about the situation. Despite explaining the difficulties we faced, we were informed that no credit or refund could be provided.

Having booked a total of four campsites, our group, including elderly family members and young kids, struggled to fully utilize our reserved period due to the weather conditions. It was disheartening to not receive any consideration, given the circumstances.

While I understand campsite policies may not typically allow for refunds or credits under such circumstances, I believe a more empathetic approach could have greatly improved our experience. I hope the management considers these situations more individually and provides a more customer-centric solution in the future.
Great experience
Worst campsite so far in Australia, I would avoid top site at all costs .The  amenity blocks are dirty and uncared for while it is completely accessible for public in vehicles or on foot at all times

Overpriced for what it was. Our caravan barely fit in the space. The site was just dirt, which eventually turned into mud with heavy rain. Our site had a tree on one side that had limbs hanging and were brushing against the top of our caravan making it very noisy. This tree made it so we could only have the caravan facing a certain way which put our draw bar pointing into another site. If someone had come and set up in front of our draw bar we wouldn't have been able to hitch up when leaving. Thankfully no one else arrived (not sure if this is how they planned it). The park is very close to the road making it noisy, especially in the morning as bus after bus pulls into town. 10pm - 7am noise restrictions are not enforced. Had people carrying on until 1am every night. Honestly, we've stayed at a lot of 'G'day Parks' and were surprised this caravan park is part of their network.
On the bright side, the staff were very nice. The toilet and shower blocks are very clean and there was a reasonable wi-fi connection (maybe because we were next to the office). Supermarket is just across the road.

Great spot in Lorne, easy walk to Main Street. Tanbark under site next to the river was great.
Playground was dirty, hire fire place did not come with enough wood to make it worth it. Not much for kids.

Le camping possède trois sites sur Lorne, deux dans la ville et un sur les hauteurs. Réservation en ligne très simple et bon rapport qualité prix. Attention si vous arrivez après 16h00, plus personne à la réception et vous devrez disposer d'une ligne téléphonique ou d'un accès à vos mail pour recevoir les codes d'entrée... qu'ils n'envoient qu'à 16h00... un peu galère !

Bird poo all over the womens showers. Powered sites are full of dirt. So dark at night you can't see where your going if you have to walk to the toilet. Sites are very small and squashed next to other people. No privacy and not comfortable.
Really nice place,  great overnight stay,  friendly staff, very friendly wildlife ( just don't feed).
very bad experience

The location is great.  The cabin 10 was value for money in this location.  Not 5 stars but who cares, get outside and enjoy.  There was a reason it was the last one left and we got it unplanned. As a bonus was surrounded by VW's for an Amarok driver we were in heaven .
Quite and clean campsite
I only had to go  there  to do a  job  there to dear to  camp there

Booked Top Bank sites  and was not explained by staff that it is a road with 150 cars a day plus driving past , worst site ever, management should explain this on booking , i thought i was getting a caravan park site not an fing site next to a gravel road with no one observing the 10klms an hour limit. disgusting.
I don't usually leave bad reviews however this caravan park is terrible. We are on site 34 at the top. Dirt site, toilets and showers terrible. Do not stay here. Terrible.

3 fair stars. We stayed at the topbank. The site is very small and I recommend to NOT stay site number 30 or below. The park is nicer at the back. Toilet blocks look neglected. No Kitchen available. Check in was easy enough. Staff members aren't overly friendly. The only + is the location of the topbank. 1 min to the beach and 3 minutes into town.
Best cabin is so clean and comfortable
Nice and quiet this time of the year. Busier on weekend in the township. Close walking distance to main street.
Only thing I found was the bed was very cold needed a electric blanket

Wow, $45 für 2 Personen für eine unpowered site. Auf einem vergleichbaren Campingplatz an der Great Ocean Road zahlten wir die Hälfte!
(Auto+Dachzelt)
Bueno
Site was a bit muddy but amenities were good. Great location
Quiet park walking distance to town
It's too noisy for camping sites cause it is right next to the road. Also, the toilet in the morning they don't have light on

Arrived to be given the most smelliest site just couldn't get over how much duck poo was around and right near the bridge where traffic cross over river changed sites to a better one,  I personally would be getting my maintance man raking each site and clearing the duck poo of rhe sites each day or at least get it once tge caravan books it site and due to come its just common sense and hygiene to do so
The used linen was stacked outside the front building there were so many used bags of linen, which to me is such a no no for hygiene purposes and not a good look fir the caravan park poor people in the on site cabins open your window to see that  it should have been behind closed doors away from public
The staff were good and very helpful and accommodating The toilets amenities were nice and clean
Would I stay again no just wasn't the caravan park for me.
